channel.title: NHC Atlantic
channel.description: Active tropical cyclones in the Atlantic, Caribbean, and the Gulf of America

item.title: Atlantic Tropical Weather Outlook
item.description:
000
ABNT20 KNHC 230525
TWOAT

Tropical Weather Outlook
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL
200 AM EDT Thu Jul 23 2026

For the North Atlantic...Caribbean Sea and the Gulf of America:

Active Systems:
The National Hurricane Center is issuing advisories on Tropical
Storm Bertha, just offshore of the southern Louisiana coast.

Tropical cyclone formation is not expected during the next 7 days.

$$
Forecaster Kelly
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Summary for Tropical Storm Bertha (AT2/AL022026)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
item.description: ...BERTHA NOW SOUTH OF THE COAST OF SOUTHERN LOUISIANA... ...WARNINGS DISCONTINUED FOR PORTIONS OF THE LOUISIANA AND MISSISSIPPI COASTS... As of 1:00 AM CDT Thu Jul 23 the center of Bertha was located near 29.1, -91.6 with movement W at 12 mph. The minimum central pressure was 1003 mb with maximum sustained winds of about 45 mph.

nhc:Cyclone.nhc:name: Bertha
nhc:Cyclone.nhc:headline: ...BERTHA NOW SOUTH OF THE COAST OF SOUTHERN LOUISIANA... ...WARNINGS DISCONTINUED FOR PORTIONS OF THE LOUISIANA AND MISSISSIPPI COASTS...

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Public Advisory Number 15a
item.description:
Issued at 100 AM CDT Thu Jul 23 2026 
000
WTNT32 KNHC 230535
TCPAT2
 
BULLETIN
Tropical Storm Bertha Intermediate Advisory Number 15A
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       AL022026
100 AM CDT Thu Jul 23 2026
 
...BERTHA NOW SOUTH OF THE COAST OF SOUTHERN LOUISIANA...
...WARNINGS DISCONTINUED FOR PORTIONS OF THE LOUISIANA AND 
MISSISSIPPI COASTS...
 
 
SUMMARY OF 100 AM CDT...0600 UTC...INFORMATION
----------------------------------------------
LOCATION...29.1N 91.6W
ABOUT 110 MI...175 KM SW OF NEW ORLEANS LOUISIANA
ABOUT 120 MI...190 KM SE OF LAKE CHARLES LOUISIANA
MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS...45 MPH...75 KM/H
PRESENT MOVEMENT...W OR 260 DEGREES AT 12 MPH...19 KM/H
MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE...1003 MB...29.62 INCHES
 
 
WATCHES AND WARNINGS
--------------------
CHANGES WITH THIS ADVISORY:
 
The Tropical Storm Warning has been discontinued for the Louisiana 
and Mississippi coasts north and east of the Plaquemines/St. 
Bernard Parish line, including Metropolitan New Orleans and Lake 
Pontchartrain.
 
SUMMARY OF WATCHES AND WARNINGS IN EFFECT:
 
A Tropical Storm Warning is in effect for...
* The Gulf Coast from the Plaquemines/St. Bernard Parish line, 
Louisiana to Sargent, Texas
 
A Tropical Storm Warning means that tropical storm conditions are
expected somewhere within the warning area, in this case within 24
hours.
 
For storm information specific to your area, including possible
inland watches and warnings, please monitor products issued by your
local National Weather Service forecast office.
 
 
DISCUSSION AND OUTLOOK
----------------------
At 100 AM CDT (0600 UTC), the center of Tropical Storm Bertha was
located near latitude 29.1 North, longitude 91.6 West. Bertha is
moving toward the west near 12 mph (19 km/h).  A generally westward 
motion is expected through Friday.  On the forecast track, the 
center of Bertha will move near or along the Louisiana and upper 
Texas coasts through this afternoon, and then move farther inland 
into Texas this evening and tonight.
 
Recent satellite wind data indicate that maximum sustained winds 
remain near 45 mph (75 km/h) with higher gusts. Little change in 
strength is expected today, and Bertha is forecast to maintain 
tropical storm intensity up until it reaches the upper Texas coast 
later today.  Bertha is expected to dissipate inland over Texas by 
tonight or Friday.
 
Tropical-storm-force winds extend outward up to 160 miles (260 km)
mainly to the south of the center. An oil platform south of the 
Louisiana coast recently reported sustained winds of 52 mph (84 
km/h) and a wind gust of 61 mph (98 km/h) at an elevation of 82 ft 
(25 m). Another oil platform located southwest of the center of 
Bertha was recently hit by a severe thunderstorm and reported a wind 
gust of 82 mph (132 km/h) at an elevation of 98 ft (30 m).
 
The estimated minimum central pressure is 1003 mb (29.62 inches).
 
 
HAZARDS AFFECTING LAND
----------------------
Key messages for Tropical Storm Bertha can be found in the Tropical
Cyclone Discussion under AWIPS header MIATCDAT2 and WMO header
WTNT42 KNHC.
 
WIND:  Tropical storm conditions will continue spreading across
portions of the northern and northwestern Gulf Coast within the
warning area through today.
 
STORM SURGE:  The combination of storm surge and the tide will
cause normally dry areas near the immediate coast to be flooded by
rising waters moving inland from the shoreline. The water could
reach the following heights above ground somewhere in the indicated
areas if the peak surge occurs at the time of high tide...
 
Alabama/Mississippi border to Port Bolivar, TX...1-3 ft
Lake Pontchartrain...1-3 ft
 
The deepest water will occur along the immediate coast.
Surge-related flooding depends on the relative timing of the surge
and the tidal cycle, and can vary greatly over short distances. For
information specific to your area, please see products issued by
your local National Weather Service forecast office.
 
For a complete depiction of areas at risk of storm surge inundation,
please see the National Weather Service Peak Storm Surge Graphic,
available at hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?peakSurge.
 
RAINFALL: Bertha is expected to produce total rainfall amounts of 2
to 4 inches with isolated totals to 6 inches through tonight along 
Texas Gulf Coast and South Texas.  This rainfall may produce 
isolated flash flooding, especially in urban areas.
 
For a complete depiction of forecast rainfall associated with
Bertha, please see the National Weather Service Storm Total Rainfall
Graphic available at hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?rainqpf and
the Flash Flood Risk graphic at
hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?ero.
 
SURF: Swells generated by Bertha will continue spreading westward
along the northern Gulf Coast during the next few days. These swells
are likely to cause life-threatening surf and rip current
conditions. Please consult products from your local weather office.
 
A depiction of rip current risk for the United States can be found
at: hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?ripCurrents
 
 
NEXT ADVISORY
-------------
Next complete advisory at 400 AM CDT.
 
$$
Forecaster Beven
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Forecast Advisory Number 15
item.description:
Issued at 0300 UTC THU JUL 23 2026 
000
WTNT22 KNHC 230242
TCMAT2

 
TROPICAL STORM BERTHA FORECAST/ADVISORY NUMBER  15
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       AL022026
0300 UTC THU JUL 23 2026
 
TROPICAL STORM CENTER LOCATED NEAR 29.4N  91.1W AT 23/0300Z
POSITION ACCURATE WITHIN  30 NM
 
PRESENT MOVEMENT TOWARD THE WEST OR 260 DEGREES AT  10 KT
 
ESTIMATED MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE 1002 MB
MAX SUSTAINED WINDS  40 KT WITH GUSTS TO  50 KT.
34 KT....... 50NE 140SE 140SW  30NW.
4 M SEAS....  0NE 120SE   0SW   0NW.
WINDS AND SEAS VARY GREATLY IN EACH QUADRANT.  RADII IN NAUTICAL
MILES ARE THE LARGEST RADII EXPECTED ANYWHERE IN THAT QUADRANT.
 
REPEAT...CENTER LOCATED NEAR 29.4N  91.1W AT 23/0300Z
AT 23/0000Z CENTER WAS LOCATED NEAR 29.4N  90.5W
 
FORECAST VALID 23/1200Z 29.2N  92.8W
MAX WIND  40 KT...GUSTS  50 KT.
34 KT... 60NE 130SE 120SW   0NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 24/0000Z 29.1N  95.6W...INLAND
MAX WIND  35 KT...GUSTS  45 KT.
34 KT... 60NE  90SE   0SW   0NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 24/1200Z 29.0N  98.3W...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
MAX WIND  25 KT...GUSTS  35 KT.
 
FORECAST VALID 25/0000Z...DISSIPATED
 
REQUEST FOR 3 HOURLY SHIP REPORTS WITHIN 300 MILES OF 29.4N  91.1W
 
INTERMEDIATE PUBLIC ADVISORY...WTNT32 KNHC/MIATCPAT2...AT 23/0600Z
 
NEXT ADVISORY AT 23/0900Z
 
$$
FORECASTER PASCH
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Forecast Discussion Number 15
item.description:
Issued at 1000 PM CDT Wed Jul 22 2026 
000
WTNT42 KNHC 230248
TCDAT2
 
Tropical Storm Bertha Discussion Number  15
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       AL022026
1000 PM CDT Wed Jul 22 2026
 
After making landfall in southeastern Louisiana earlier today,
Surface observations show that the center has become less well
defined.  It appears, however, that the center has slipped a little
southwestward and close to the coast of south Louisiana.  Most of 
the deep convection continues to be south and southwest of the 
center, out over the northern Gulf, due to the persistent 
north-northeasterly vertical wind shear.  The overall cloud pattern 
is not very well organized, and is elongated from southwest to 
northeast.  The intensity estimate is held at 40 kt for this 
advisory, with these winds occurring primarily over the Gulf waters.

Bertha has been moving a little south of west over the past several 
hours, with the initial motion a faster 260/10 kt.  The main 
steering mechanism continues to be a large and strong 
mid-tropospheric anticyclone over the south-central United States.  
Bertha should move along the southern periphery of this high 
pressure area for the next day or two, and is expected to briefly 
re-emerge over the northwestern Gulf waters on Thursday. The system 
should cross the upper Texas coast later on Thursday and move 
inland over Texas after that.  The official forecast track is just 
slightly south of the previous NHC prediction, mainly due to the 
more southern location of the center, and is close to the latest 
corrected consensus, HCCA, guidance.

Since the storm should remain in a dry mid-level air mass with 
continued strong north-northeasterly vertical wind shear through 
Thursday, significant re-strengthening over the northwestern Gulf 
waters is unlikely.  The official intensity forecast is on the high 
side of the model guidance, close to the SHIPS prediction.
 
 
Key Messages:
 
1. Tropical storm conditions are expected to spread westward along
the northern Gulf Coast from Mississippi to southwestern Louisiana,
including the Metro New Orleans area, tonight and on Thursday.
Tropical storm conditions are expected over portions of the upper 
Texas coast on Thursday.
 
2. A Coastal Flood Warning is in effect for the Mississippi and
Louisiana coastlines, where moderate coastal flooding is expected.
 
3. Isolated flash flooding is possible along the Texas Gulf Coast 
and South Texas through Thursday night.
 
 
FORECAST POSITIONS AND MAX WINDS
 
INIT  23/0300Z 29.4N  91.1W   40 KT  45 MPH
 12H  23/1200Z 29.2N  92.8W   40 KT  45 MPH
 24H  24/0000Z 29.1N  95.6W   35 KT  40 MPH...INLAND
 36H  24/1200Z 29.0N  98.3W   25 KT  30 MPH...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
 48H  25/0000Z...DISSIPATED
 
$$
Forecaster Pasch
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Wind Speed Probabilities Number 15
item.description:
Issued at 0300 UTC THU JUL 23 2026 
000
FONT12 KNHC 230243
PWSAT2
                                                                    
TROPICAL STORM BERTHA WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES NUMBER  15           
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       AL022026               
0300 UTC THU JUL 23 2026                                            
                                                                    
AT 0300Z THE CENTER OF TROPICAL STORM BERTHA WAS LOCATED NEAR       
LATITUDE 29.4 NORTH...LONGITUDE 91.1 WEST WITH MAXIMUM SUSTAINED    
WINDS NEAR 40 KTS...45 MPH...75 KM/H.                               
                                                                    
Z INDICATES COORDINATED UNIVERSAL TIME (GREENWICH)                  
   ATLANTIC STANDARD TIME (AST)...SUBTRACT 4 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   EASTERN  DAYLIGHT TIME (EDT)...SUBTRACT 4 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   CENTRAL  DAYLIGHT TIME (CDT)...SUBTRACT 5 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
                                                                    
WIND SPEED PROBABILITY TABLE FOR SPECIFIC LOCATIONS                 
                                                                    
CHANCES OF SUSTAINED (1-MINUTE AVERAGE) WIND SPEEDS OF AT LEAST     
   ...34 KT (39 MPH... 63 KM/H)...                                  
   ...50 KT (58 MPH... 93 KM/H)...                                  
   ...64 KT (74 MPH...119 KM/H)...                                  
FOR LOCATIONS AND TIME PERIODS DURING THE NEXT 5 DAYS               
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES FOR LOCATIONS ARE GIVEN AS OP(CP) WHERE               
    OP  IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT BEGINNING DURING            
        AN INDIVIDUAL TIME PERIOD (ONSET PROBABILITY)               
   (CP) IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT OCCURRING BETWEEN           
        00Z THU AND THE FORECAST HOUR (CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY)      
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES ARE GIVEN IN PERCENT                                  
X INDICATES PROBABILITIES LESS THAN 1 PERCENT                       
PROBABILITIES FOR 34 KT AND 50 KT ARE SHOWN AT A GIVEN LOCATION WHEN
THE 5-DAY CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 3 PERCENT.             
PROBABILITIES FOR 34...50...64 KT SHOWN WHEN THE 5-DAY              
64-KT CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 1 PERCENT.                 
                                                                    
                                                                    
  - - - - WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES FOR SELECTED LOCATIONS - - - -   
                                                                    
               FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM 
  TIME       00Z THU 12Z THU 00Z FRI 12Z FRI 00Z SAT 00Z SUN 00Z MON
PERIODS         TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO  
             12Z THU 00Z FRI 12Z FRI 00Z SAT 00Z SUN 00Z MON 00Z TUE
                                                                    
FORECAST HOUR    (12)   (24)    (36)    (48)    (72)    (96)   (120)
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - 
LOCATION       KT                                                   
                                                                    
BURAS LA       34 99   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)
 
GFAM 280N 890W 34 83   X(83)   X(83)   X(83)   X(83)   X(83)   X(83)
 
NEW ORLEANS LA 34 24   X(24)   X(24)   X(24)   X(24)   X(24)   X(24)
 
HOUMA LA       34 98   X(98)   X(98)   X(98)   X(98)   X(98)   X(98)
 
GFAM 280N 910W 34 99   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)
 
BATON ROUGE LA 34  3   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)
 
MORGAN CITY LA 34 90   X(90)   X(90)   X(90)   X(90)   X(90)   X(90)
 
LAFAYETTE LA   34 10   X(10)   X(10)   X(10)   X(10)   X(10)   X(10)
 
NEW IBERIA LA  34 20   X(20)   X(20)   X(20)   X(20)   X(20)   X(20)
 
GFAM 280N 930W 34 70   2(72)   X(72)   X(72)   X(72)   X(72)   X(72)
 
LAKE CHARLES   34  4   3( 7)   X( 7)   X( 7)   X( 7)   X( 7)   X( 7)
 
CAMERON LA     34 19  23(42)   X(42)   X(42)   X(42)   X(42)   X(42)
CAMERON LA     50  1   2( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)
 
KOUNTZE TX     34  X   3( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)
 
PORT ARTHUR TX 34  2  10(12)   X(12)   X(12)   X(12)   X(12)   X(12)
 
GALVESTON TX   34  2  56(58)   X(58)   X(58)   X(58)   X(58)   X(58)
GALVESTON TX   50  X   5( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)
 
HOUSTON TX     34  X  13(13)   1(14)   X(14)   X(14)   X(14)   X(14)
 
SAN ANTONIO TX 34  X   X( X)   6( 6)   X( 6)   X( 6)   X( 6)   X( 6)
 
FREEPORT TX    34  X  52(52)   2(54)   X(54)   X(54)   X(54)   X(54)
 
GFAM 280N 950W 34  1  39(40)   X(40)   X(40)   X(40)   X(40)   X(40)
 
HIGH ISLAND TX 34  3  33(36)   X(36)   X(36)   X(36)   X(36)   X(36)

MATAGORDA TX   50  X   3( 3)   1( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)
 
PORT O CONNOR  34  X  28(28)  10(38)   X(38)   X(38)   X(38)   X(38)
 
ROCKPORT TX    34  X   6( 6)  12(18)   X(18)   X(18)   X(18)   X(18)
 
CORPUS CHRISTI 34  X   1( 1)   7( 8)   X( 8)   X( 8)   X( 8)   X( 8)
 
GFAM 270N 960W 34  X   3( 3)   1( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)
 
$$                                                                  
FORECASTER PASCH
